last_item: Band-1.5: restore-test DEEP proof — Manager.RestoreTestDeep does a non-destructive scratch restore + boot (full WAL replay) + real row count, the only thing that catches recovery-time failures `pgbackrest verify` cannot
last_result: done (CORE) — added Manager.RestoreTestDeep (internal/backup/restore_deep.go): restores newest backup into a fresh os.MkdirTemp scratch dir (--pg1-path override, NEVER live data dir), boots via pg_ctl on a PRIVATE socket (listen_addresses= empty → no TCP, unix_socket_directories=<scratch>, archive_mode=off in BOTH restore config + boot opts → can't archive into live repo), counts user-table rows into verified_rows, ALWAYS tears down (deferred stop + RemoveAll, detached ctx). Guards: foreign-owner HARD STOP, no-backup→NotFound, disk-headroom precheck (free >= dbSize×1.25 via injectable diskFree/statfs; MkdirAll scratch root first) that refuses CodeSafety + issues NO restore when tight (a restore≈DB-size could fill the box → live-dir data loss). PG binaries resolved from restored PG_VERSION (validated). Opt-in: POST /backups/restore-test?deep=true (default stays cheap verify). Three injectable seams on Manager (scratchRoot via Options.ScratchRoot, diskFree, resolvePGBin) keep it all unit-testable via fake Runner. Reviewer (feature-dev:code-reviewer) found 2 vacuous test assertions + a space-unsafe socket arg — fixed before commit: start/stop matched on distinct tokens, socket isolation compared to EXACT scratch dir, single-quoted unix_socket_directories, MkdirAll scratch root. Full Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build); no web changes. SPLIT OUT as own band-1.5 items: real end-to-end integration test (needs a populated pgBackRest repo) + UI opt-in button.
notes: Band 1.5 (data durability) NEARLY DONE. Done: (1) 'last good backup was N ago' surfaced. (2) backup-failed/backup-stale rules + metrics + Collector.enrichBackup. (3) telemetry+alert evaluation LOOP wired (background.go). (4) SCHEDULED BACKUPS RUN: full+incremental cron jobs; Manager.backupMu single-flight = CodeConflict skip on overlap (no fail row/false alert). (5) off-host S3 nudge test-locked. (6) restore-test SURFACING (restoreTestStatus + RestoreTestStatus banner). (7) ***restore-test EXECUTION DONE: Manager.RestoreTest runs `pgbackrest verify` (VerifyCmd, command.go verifyTimeout=2h); handler wired; recordRestoreTest helper mirrors recordBackup; verify is READ-ONLY so it does NOT take backupMu (correct — won't corrupt). verify ≠ full restore, so verified_rows stays 0 (honest).*** (8) ***restore-test JOB WIRED (iter 18): startBackgroundJobs now registers a `restore-test` cron job on cfg.Schedules.RestoreTest via a generic registerJob helper; scheduledRestoreTest calls Manager.RestoreTest directly and deliberately SKIPS ensureBackupConfigured (its stanza-create exclusive lock + possible PG restart would collide with a long full backup still running at 05:00) — verify is a pure reader.*** (9) ***restore-test DEEP proof CORE DONE (iter 19): Manager.RestoreTestDeep — non-destructive scratch restore (os.MkdirTemp, --pg1-path override ≠ live dir) + pg_ctl boot (full WAL replay, private socket, archive_mode=off) + real row count into verified_rows + guaranteed teardown (deferred stop+RemoveAll). Headroom-gated (free>=dbSize×1.25, CodeSafety refusal issues no restore). Opt-in POST /backups/restore-test?deep=true. Unit-tested via fake Runner with injectable seams (scratchRoot/diskFree/resolvePGBin). Reviewer-fixed 2 vacuous assertions + space-safe socket quoting.*** REMAINING 1.5: (a) DEEP end-to-end INTEGRATION test (needs a populated pgBackRest repo + real PG to prove restore+boot+count against real binaries — the one thing fake-Runner can't cover; integration-tagged, env-gated like readonly_integration_test.go). (b) DEEP UI opt-in button on Backups page (calls ?deep=true; verified_rows>0 'rows restored and verified' branch already renders). (c) the `digest` job is still unregistered, but NO digest builder exists yet — not load-bearing for 'never lose data', defer to band 2+. After (a)+(b), band 1.5 is complete → move to band 2 (stability). Optionally wire OTLP exporter (nil in collector). Plumbing: alert engine reads Snapshot FIELDS via alert/metrics.go metricValue. Backups: internal/backup/manager.go (Backup takes backupMu.TryLock→ownership→terminal fail/success; RestoreTest verifyForRead→VerifyCmd→pass/fail row); internal/store/backup.go (ListBackups/ListRestoreTests newest-first, LatestSuccessfulBackup, InsertRestoreTest). Frontend SUCCESS_RESULTS={success,ok,completed,pass}/FAILURE_RESULTS={fail,failed,error} in Backups.tsx — Manager writes 'success'/'fail'. Scheduler: internal/scheduler — empty spec disables. go/psql/pg_ctl snap-sandboxed (run sandbox-disabled). TMPDIR empty in some shells — use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
